title: Week 32
summary:
Capesize
A strong week for the big ships but rates edged lower as the week closed out largely on the back of falling paper values and a long weekend holiday in Singapore. The West Australia/China run reached a peak of $7.75 briefly but within same day drifted to the mid $7.00s and then dropped to $7.35 basis Port Hedland. Midweek a 175,500 tonner open Fangcheng achieved $23,000 daily for a West Australian round but rates have since eased.

children.title: Capesize
children.summary: A strong week for the big ships but rates edged lower as the week closed out largely on the back of falling paper values and a long weekend holiday in Singapore. The West Australia/China run reached a peak of $7.75 briefly but within same day drifted to the mid $7.00s and then dropped to $7.35 basis Port Hedland.

children.title: Panamax
children.summary: Thin volume once again in the Atlantic with tonnage still tight on the north Continent with charterers paying a premium for the short Baltic rounds. Midweek a 2006 built kamsarmax fixed a Baltic round at $14,000 daily while a post panamax fixed from Lulea via the Baltic and Mediterranean with Gibraltar redelivery at $14,500 daily.

children.title: Supramax / Handysize
children.summary: The Supamax market remained strong in nearly all sectors. Atlantic rates continued to outstrip the Far East with owners preparing to take discounts to re-position.
